title: Trump seeks deal to neutralize Iran's highly enriched uranium stockpile
subhead: The US president claims he can secure a better deal than the 2015 agreement signed by Barack Obama, but Tehran is refusing to grant Washington even a symbolic victory on the issue.

title: Heatwave in France: 'This is an unprecedented event, with about a one-in-1,000 chance of occurring in a given year'
Raphaël Glucksmann tests his presidential prospects
Diplomacy 'is not calling everyone an antisemite': Israeli opposition outlines foreign policy vision
subhead: Former prime ministers Yair Lapid and Naftali Bennett unveiled the initial pillars of the foreign policy that the two main rivals to Benjamin Netanyahu pledge to pursue, ahead of the general elections scheduled for the fall.

title: French Open: Moïse Kouamé, 17, makes a splash on big debut
subhead: The Frenchman made a successful Grand Slam debut by defeating Croatia's Marin Cilic in the first round (7-6, 6-2, 6-1) on Tuesday. He is the youngest player to reach the second round since 1991.

title: Sonny Rollins, giant of jazz and the saxophone, has died
subhead: The creator of some 20 albums that are landmarks in the history of jazz, the former collaborator of John Coltrane, Charlie Parker and Miles Davis pioneered a unique form of marathon concert. He died on May 25 at the age of 95.

title: 'Believing they already serve humanity, Musk, Bezos and Thiel see no need for philanthropy'
subhead: With his foundation, Bill Gates embodied philanthropy. But his ties to Jeffrey Epstein have tarnished his reputation, and the new tech tycoons now place more faith in themselves than in governments to improve humanity, writes Le Monde's Arnaud Leparmentier in his column.

How China uses influencers to polish its image
subhead: Chongqing, a futuristic Chinese city, has caught the eye of influencers and bloggers. Beijing, meanwhile, welcomes new visitors, often unaware of the geopolitical and ideological currents running beneath their social media posts.

title: Why dropping the worst-case climate scenario does not undermine the reality of global warming
subhead: A new generation of climate scenarios has set aside RCP8.5, which projected a nearly 5°C rise in global temperatures by 2100. Donald Trump presents this as proof of 'alarmism' among climate scientists, but the new projections remain highly concerning.

title: The post-May '68 Christian philosophy now being embraced by the American far right
subhead: Trump supporters often blame the cultural upheavals triggered by the May 1968 protests in France for sowing the seeds of 'wokeness.' Yet some of them are now claiming a different French school of thought, which took shape at Stanford University under the influence of René Girard.
